description: Polish pass — fix windgram visual quality, interaction bugs, test infrastructure, and tech debt. Goal: a paragliding pilot visits the site, sees an intuitive and useful windgram that looks professional and works smoothly.

context.stack: Next.js 16 + Turso + shadcn/ui + Tailwind v4 + Canvas-based windgram
context.reference: Compare our windgram against the RASP windgrams at http://wxtofly.net/v2/windgrams.html — those are the gold standard for readability. Rich purple/orange lapse rate fills, white wind barbs on colored background, clear grid, high information density without clutter.
currentIssues: Windgram background is mostly white — lapse rate coloring is barely visible. Should have rich fills like the reference.
Numbers at top of windgram all show ~30 — these should be thermal velocity (W* in m/s), not wind speed.
Wind barbs are small and hard to read. Need better sizing and color contrast.
Y-axis labels are cramped (pressure/altitude text overlapping).
Hover/rollover on the chart feels glitchy — crosshair jumps, detail panel pops in/out erratically.
Day selector shows stale date (Mar 6 when it's Mar 7) — should default to today.
Test file mobile-responsive.spec.ts uses Playwright imports but lives in tests/ where Vitest picks it up — causes test failure.
No lapse rate color legend visible on the chart page.
context.designValues: Beautiful, minimalistic, useful. A pilot should be able to glance at the windgram and immediately understand: will thermals form? How strong? What altitude? What winds? When is the best window?

userStories.title: Fix Lapse Rate Background — Rich Color Fills
userStories.description: The windgram background should have vivid, meaningful color fills showing atmospheric stability at every altitude/time cell. Currently it's mostly white/transparent. Reference RASP windgrams have rich purple (stable/inversion) and orange/red (unstable) fills covering the ENTIRE chart area.
acceptanceCriteria: Every cell in the windgram has a background color based on lapse rate — no white/empty areas

Color scale: deep purple/blue for inversions (lapse rate < 0°C/1000ft), lavender/light purple for stable (0-1.5°C), pale/white for neutral (~2°C), warm yellow for conditionally unstable (2-2.5°C), orange/red for unstable (>3°C/1000ft)
Colors are smooth between cells — use bilinear interpolation for gradient effect
The chart should look like a continuous colored field, not a grid of squares
A visible color legend on the chart or below it showing: Inversion → Stable → Neutral → Unstable with corresponding colors
Dark mode: colors should still be readable, adjust alpha/brightness if needed
Verify lapse rate computation is correct: (T_lower - T_upper) / (height_upper - height_lower) * 304.8 for °C/1000ft
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The lapse rate computation in lapse-rate-utils.ts may be returning wrong values or the color mapping thresholds may be off. Debug by logging actual lapse rate values for a real profile. The dry adiabatic lapse rate (~3°C/1000ft) should map to the transition from neutral to unstable. Check that bilinearInterpolate is actually being called and the fillRect calls use the interpolated colors, not just cell-center colors.

userStories.title: Fix Thermal Velocity Display at Top
userStories.description: The numbers at the top of the windgram currently show ~30 for every hour. These should show thermal updraft velocity (W*) in m/s, not wind speed. This is one of the most important pieces of info for a pilot.
acceptanceCriteria: Top row shows thermal velocity (W*) in m/s for each hour, NOT wind speed
Values should vary through the day: 0 early morning, increasing to peak in afternoon, back to 0 by evening
Color-coded: 0 m/s = gray, <1 = light blue, 1-2 = green, 2-3 = orange, >3 = red

If thermal velocity data is not available in the atmospheric profile, compute it from CAPE and boundary layer height: W* = (g * BLH * surface_heat_flux / (air_density * Cp * surface_temp))^(1/3), or use simplified: W* ≈ 0.12 * sqrt(CAPE) as approximation
Label clearly: 'W*' or 'Thermal' above the values
npm run build succeeds
Unit test for thermal velocity calculation
userStories.notes: Check thermal-cloud-utils.ts drawThermalIndicator function. The current implementation may be displaying wind speed at some level instead of derived thermal velocity. The atmospheric profile has cape and boundary_layer_height in surface data which can be used to estimate W*.

userStories.title: Improve Wind Barbs — Size, Density, Readability
userStories.description: Wind barbs should be the signature visual element overlaid on the lapse rate background. Currently they're too small and hard to read.
acceptanceCriteria: Wind barbs are larger — shaft length ~20-25px, clearly visible at chart scale
White barb color on colored backgrounds (like RASP reference) for contrast, OR color-coded by speed with enough contrast against the lapse rate background
Standard meteorological convention: half barb = 5 kt, full barb = 10 kt, pennant = 50 kt
On mobile (<640px): show barbs at every other pressure level and every other hour to avoid clutter
On desktop: show barbs at every level and hour
Barb rotation correctly shows wind direction (shaft points in direction wind comes FROM)
Hover tooltip shows: wind speed (kt and km/h), direction (degrees and cardinal), altitude
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: wind-barb-utils.ts has the drawing logic. Check that the canvas scaling / DPI handling isn't shrinking the barbs. The barbs should be drawn AFTER the lapse rate background so they sit on top.

userStories.title: Fix Chart Layout — Y-Axis Labels, Spacing, Polish
userStories.description: Clean up the chart layout: Y-axis labels overlap, padding isn't right, and the overall feel needs to be more polished.
acceptanceCriteria: Y-axis left side: pressure (hPa) and altitude (m) on separate lines, clearly readable
Y-axis right side: altitude in feet, aligned properly
X-axis: time labels (6 AM, 8 AM, etc.) evenly spaced, not crowded
Chart padding adjusted so labels never overlap the chart area or get cut off
Grid lines are subtle (thin, low opacity) — not competing with data
Canvas renders at correct DPI for retina displays (window.devicePixelRatio)
Chart aspect ratio works on mobile (taller relative to width) and desktop (wider)
The chart border/container looks clean — rounded corners, subtle shadow
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The left axis currently shows '1000hPa/411m' crammed together. Split into two lines or use only the most useful label. Consider: pressure levels on the left, altitude (m and ft) on the right. Check that canvas width/height attributes match the CSS dimensions × devicePixelRatio.

userStories.title: Fix Crosshair/Hover Interaction
userStories.description: The hover behavior on the windgram is glitchy — crosshair jumps around, detail panel appears/disappears erratically. Make it smooth and useful.
acceptanceCriteria: Crosshair follows pointer smoothly without jumping
Crosshair drawn as thin vertical + horizontal lines spanning the full chart area
Crosshair snaps to nearest hour column (vertical) and is free on altitude (horizontal)
Detail panel appears on hover after brief delay (100ms debounce) — no flickering
On desktop: detail panel in a fixed side panel (right), not following the cursor
On mobile: detail panel as a fixed bottom sheet, not covering the chart
Click to pin crosshair, click again or Escape to unpin
When pinned, moving mouse off chart doesn't close the detail panel
Pointer leave: crosshair disappears after 200ms delay (prevents flicker when moving between elements)
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The current implementation uses onPointerMove/onPointerLeave directly on the canvas. Add debouncing and smooth transitions. The detail panel should NOT use position:fixed following the cursor — it should be in a stable position. Check that pointer coordinates are correctly translated to chart coordinates accounting for canvas scaling.

userStories.title: Fix Day Selector — Default to Today
userStories.description: The day selector should default to today's date, not a stale cached date.
acceptanceCriteria: Day selector defaults to today's date on page load
If today's data is not available (e.g., after midnight before new forecast), default to the first available day
Selected day tab is clearly highlighted (current styling is good, just fix the default)
Switching days is instant — no loading state if data is already fetched (it's all in one atmospheric profile)
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The issue is likely that the profile cache has stale data from yesterday. Check the TTL on atmospheric_profiles_cache and ensure the day index 0 actually corresponds to today's date, not yesterday's cached data. The WindgramInteractive component should find today's date in the days array and set that as the initial selectedDayIndex.

userStories.title: Fix Test Infrastructure
userStories.description: The test suite has a failure because Playwright test files are in the wrong directory. Fix test organization so Vitest and Playwright don't collide.
acceptanceCriteria: Vitest runs with zero failures: npm run test -- --run
Playwright E2E tests are in e2e/ directory ONLY (not in tests/)
Move tests/mobile-responsive.spec.ts to e2e/mobile-responsive.spec.ts
Vitest config excludes e2e/ and **/e2e/** from test matching
Playwright config points to e2e/ directory for test files
Delete any empty or orphaned test directories
Existing unit tests in src/lib/__tests__/ and src/components/windgram/*.test.ts still pass
Add a meaningful unit test: test the lapse rate color mapping with known input values
npm run test -- --run passes with zero failures
npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: The issue is clear: tests/mobile-responsive.spec.ts imports from @playwright/test but Vitest tries to run it. Either move it to e2e/ or add tests/ to vitest exclude. Prefer moving it since it's an E2E test.

userStories.title: Site Detail Page UX Polish
userStories.description: The site detail page should feel intuitive for a paragliding pilot. Clean up the information hierarchy, fix the orientations display, and make the page flow naturally.
acceptanceCriteria: Orientations display uses wind compass rose or simple badges, not 'N✓✓, NE✓' text
Ideal wind directions shown as colored arrows or clear badges
Elevation shown with units toggle (m/ft) that remembers preference
Map is smaller (not taking half the page) — the windgram is the hero
Source attribution is subtle (small text, not prominent)
'Add to Favorites' button is visible but not dominant
Page loads fast: site info shows immediately, windgram loads progressively
The heading hierarchy is clean: Site name > Flyability summary > Windgram > Details
Mobile: everything stacks naturally, no horizontal scroll
npm run build succeeds
npm run lint passes
userStories.notes: Currently the orientations show as 'N✓✓, SE✓, S✓✓' which is confusing. Consider a small compass rose SVG or simple colored badges like 'N ●● NE ● SE ● S ●●' where ●● = good, ● = ok. The map takes too much vertical space — make it shorter or collapsible. The windgram should be visible without scrolling on desktop.
